meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy for Freelancers: Advanced Tutorial
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing models to overcome lack of SaaS knowledge. A professional-grade tutorial for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code for speed.
content.answer_block: Stop guessing your pricing tiers. Leverage value-based pricing by mapping user outcomes to feature gates. Use Antigravity to scaffold your billing schema and Claude Code to implement Stripe webhooks, ensuring your SaaS architecture scales from day one without needing deep domain expertise or legacy backend experience.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank database schema, the deadline is breathing down your neck, and the client is asking for a 'flexible pricing model' that doesn't exist yet. The gap between your technical skill and business logic is where most freelance projects bleed profit. You don't need a business degree; you need a system.

We are going to bridge that gap using Antigravity to model your SaaS architecture and Claude Code to inject production-ready billing logic. This isn't just about code; it's about building a repeatable pricing engine that makes you look like a seasoned CTO to every client you serve.

steps.title: Define the Value-Based Pricing Model
steps.description: Before writing code, map your features to user personas. Use Antigravity to structure the pricing tiers as a clear data model.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema for a SaaS pricing model with three tiers: Starter, Pro, and Enterprise. Include fields for feature flags, monthly vs. annual pricing, and usage-based limits.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate pricing-schema.sql based on the Antigravity output'

steps.title: Bootstrap Stripe Integration
steps.description: Use Claude Code to interface with the Stripe API to handle the checkout session logic, ensuring it maps to your previously defined tiers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript interface for a Stripe checkout handler that maps our pricing tier IDs to Stripe Product IDs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'stripe-sdk' && claude run 'implement checkout session creation'

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating Logic
steps.description: Create a middleware that checks the user's subscription status against the database before granting access to specific features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust middleware function in Node.js that checks if the current user's subscription tier allows access to a specific feature flag.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create auth-guard.middleware.ts for feature gating'

steps.title: Setup Webhook Event Handlers
steps.description: Handle subscription lifecycle events like cancellations and upgrades to keep your app in sync with Stripe.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a webhook handler for Stripe events including customer.subscription.updated and invoice.payment_succeeded.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement stripe-webhook.controller.ts and update user subscription status'

steps.title: Automate Tier Upgrades/Downgrades
steps.description: Ensure the transition between tiers is seamless for the user, preventing churn through automation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft logic to handle prorated billing when a user upgrades from Starter to Pro mid-month.

pro_tips: Always use UUIDs for pricing plan identifiers so you can refactor your database without breaking Stripe sync.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your middleware to avoid locking out users for 24 hours after a failed credit card payment.
Use metadata fields in Stripe to store your internal application user ID for seamless reconciliation.
Never hardcode pricing values in the frontend; always fetch them from the API to ensure real-time consistency.
Add a 'Trial Expired' flag to your user object to trigger automated email sequences via your CRM.
content.next_steps: Deploy your staging environment to Vercel or Railway, run a test suite against the Stripe CLI, and then present the working dashboard to your client.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-grade pricing engine that rivals enterprise SaaS products. Don't just build features; build businesses. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
